themeWords.meaning: to trust, feel secure, be confident

sentenceTheme: David cries out over prolonged silence and sorrow, yet chooses to trust in the LORD’s steadfast mercy and rejoice in His salvation.

verses.text: How long wilt thou forget me, O LORD? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?

highlightedWords.meaning: to forget, neglect, cease to care
the proper name of the God of Israel

verses.text: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart daily? how long shall mine enemy be exalted over me?
Consider and hear me, O LORD my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
Lest mine enemy say, I have prevailed against him; and those that trouble me rejoice when I am moved.

highlightedWords.meaning: to totter, shake, slip, be moved

verses.text: But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.

highlightedWords.meaning: to trust, feel secure, rely upon

verses.text: I will sing unto the LORD, because he hath dealt bountifully with me.

highlightedWords.meaning: to sing, chant, celebrate in song
the proper name of the God of Israel
